blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: You're here?
Neide threw his bag aside and jumped onto the sofa.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Ugh, I'm exhausted. The road from the central region to the south is so rough.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I came from the capital.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Do you think I came in a family carriage? I traveled without food and holding in bathroom breaks. If it had been a Merkodine family carriage, I could have eaten and... well, you know.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Or done both at the same time.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Enough! Anyway, how have you been? You didn't betray us and do some special training, did you?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I didn't deny it.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Well, now that I'm here, I should aim to graduate, right?
Neide jumped up from the sofa.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Really? Damn, if you do that, what about me? What kind of magic is it? Better than Cancellation?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's still at the theoretical stage, so it's not really magic yet. As long as I complete it before the graduation exam, it'll be fine. I'll tell you once I make some progress.
Every senior plans their physical rhythm to peak on the day of the exam.
No matter the process, the result is everything, as seen with Amy.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Ugh, lucky you. I just played around during the break.
Iruki looked at Neide with a bittersweet expression.
Despite his words, he didn't seem particularly upset.
Even as a senior, it was clear Neide still had no interest in becoming a mage.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: But you...

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm? What?
Iruki shook his head and swallowed his words.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Never mind, it's nothing.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Ugh, you're so bland.
The door swung open more violently than before, and Shirone entered.
A smile bloomed on his face as he reunited with his friends.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey! What's that smell?
But his good mood was short-lived, and he quickly covered his nose in disgust.
The research club room was a mess, littered with trash. How could these guys stand the smell?
Neide and Iruki looked at each other and laughed.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Sorry. I sprayed some cologne after a long time. Did you like it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shut up! Let's clean up first!
The three of them grabbed cleaning tools and started a major cleanup.
By the time the place, which had looked like a giant trash dump, was somewhat clean, it was almost time for the event.
Exhausted, Neide lay on the sofa and looked at the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: What time is it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: One o'clock. We should start getting ready.
Shirone suddenly remembered and jumped up from the sofa.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's right! I haven't picked up my uniform!

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: I got it this morning. Yours and Neide's.
Neide gave a thumbs up.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: You're always so thorough. But do we really have to wear that? I really don't want to.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why do we have to wear uniforms for the entrance ceremony? I'd rather watch myself apply hemorrhoid cream in the mirror.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Shirone looked at Neide and asked, You have hemorrhoids?

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: It's just a saying! Anyway, let's get ready. Where's my uniform, I mean, my hemorrhoid cream?
Iruki pointed to the corner. Three boxes were stacked like a tower.
They each took a box with their name on it and unfolded the uniforms.
The white robes with stripes along the shoulders fluttered like ghosts.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Ugh, we have to meet the Class Two kids in this? They don't wear uniforms. We'll be a laughingstock.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: We have no choice. It's the rule.
Iruki didn't care much. As long as the clothes covered the important parts, like the head, eyes, and heart, he was fine.
The three of them stood in front of the mirror, wearing their uniforms over their clothes. They looked like three blobs of liquid monsters.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Will we ever get to wear these again?
With the busy schedule of senior year, there were few opportunities to wear the uniform. If they didn't pass the graduation exam, this would be the last time they saw themselves in these robes.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It won't be easy. We lack information.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Really? I think you and Shirone will definitely pass.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's just a feeling. Mathematically, it's one-third. If Shirone, Dante, and Amy are there, two will fail. If Shirone passes, Dante and Amy can't graduate.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: No way. All three could pass.
Neide couldn't imagine any of them failing.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: That's because you know them. People always think their information is the most accurate. But if that were true, no one would fail. Even on average, only 3.3 students per class pass. And we're in the lowest Class Three.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Neide couldn't argue.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Looks like everyone is here. Let's get going and get this over with.
Shirone wasn't the type to take the lead, so Dante took charge.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: His position as the top-ranked student in the advanced class helped him command the group.
Of course, once they passed through the Iron Gate, the advanced class rankings would no longer matter.
The Class Three students headed towards the senior building.
At the top of the slope stood the imposing Iron Gate.
Below it, ten Class Two students stood in a line, waiting.

blocks.speaker: Class Two Student
blocks.content: Hey, juniors! Hurry up! You're so slow!

blocks.speaker: Class Two Student
blocks.content: Nice uniforms! Are you going to a school play? Hahaha!

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Hmph! Seniors, my foot! Once we're in the senior class, we're all the same!

blocks.speaker: Closer
blocks.content: Yeah, keep talking, losers. I'll cook you all one by one.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Most students glared to avoid being intimidated, but Dante was simply bored.
As he watched his peers' reactions, he turned to look at Shirone.
He's not nervous at all.
A slight smile played on Shirone's lips, and his eyes sparkled with passion and excitement.
How could he not be thrilled? It was the scene he had imagined even in his dreams.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A mage. A mage. He would become a mage!
Fine, I'll enter hell or whatever it takes.
With each step, the Iron Gate, a symbol of wisdom, seemed to tremble.
ΓÇö Twenty minutes before the graduation event, ten students were walking towards the central park from different paths.
